title: dryopteris acuminata var. ogatana
intro: Dryopteris acuminata var. ogatana, also known as the Japanese golden fern, is a variety of fern that is native to Japan and is characterized by its golden or yellowish-green fronds. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Dryopteris acuminata var. ogatana include reducing fever, alleviating pain and inflammation, and treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant can be prepared for medicinal use through herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often consumed as teas or taken internally to harness its therapeutic benefits.
